content:
# Numbers Chapter 17 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 169-170 | **Source Image:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/NUM_017_page_001.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pa_0125-0126

---

**1** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**2** "Speak to the Sons of Israel and take from them a staff, one staff from each ancestral house -- from all their leaders according to their ancestral houses -- twelve staffs. Write each man's name on his staff.

**3** And write Aaron's name on the staff of Levi, for there shall be one staff for the head of each ancestral house.

**4** And place them in the Tent of Meeting, before the Testimony, where I meet with you.

**5** And it shall be that the man whom I choose -- his staff will sprout. And I will silence against myself the grumblings of the Sons of Israel, which they keep grumbling against you."

**6** And Moses spoke to the Sons of Israel, and all their leaders gave him a staff, one staff for each leader according to their ancestral houses -- twelve staffs. And Aaron's staff was among their staffs.

**7** And Moses placed the staffs before YHWH in the Tent of the Testimony.

**8** And on the next day Moses entered the Tent of the Testimony, and there -- Aaron's staff, for the house of Levi, had sprouted! It had put forth buds, and produced blossoms, and borne ripe almonds.

**9** And Moses brought out all the staffs from before YHWH to all the Sons of Israel, and they looked, and each man took back his own staff.

**10** And YHWH said to Moses, "Place Aaron's staff back before the Testimony, to be kept as a sign for the rebellious -- so that you may put an end, an utter end, to their grumblings against me, and they will not die."

**11** And Moses did so; just as YHWH had commanded him, so he did.

**12** And the Sons of Israel said to Moses, saying, "We are perishing! We are lost! All of us are lost!

**13** Anyone who comes near -- who comes near to the tabernacle of YHWH -- will die! Are we to be completely finished off by dying?"

---

**14** And YHWH spoke to Aaron, saying, "You and your sons and your ancestral house with you shall bear the guilt for offenses against the sanctuary. And you and your sons with you shall bear the guilt for offenses against your priesthood.

**15** And also your brothers, the tribe of Levi, the tribe of your father -- bring them near with you, and they shall be joined to you and serve you, while you and your sons with you are before the Tent of the Testimony.

**16** And they shall keep your charge and the charge of the entire Tent. But they must not come near the vessels of the sanctuary or the altar, or both they and you will die.

**17** And they shall be joined to you and keep the charge of the Tent of Meeting, for all the service of the Tent. And no outsider shall come near you.

**18** And you shall keep the charge of the sanctuary and the charge of the altar, so that there will be no more wrath against the Sons of Israel.

**19** And as for me -- I have taken your brothers the Levites from among the Sons of Israel as a gift given to you, dedicated to YHWH, to perform the service of the Tent of Meeting.

**20** And you and your sons with you shall guard your priesthood in everything concerning the altar and everything behind the curtain, and you shall serve. I give your priesthood as a gift of service, and the outsider who comes near shall be put to death."

**21** And YHWH spoke to Aaron, "And as for me -- I have given you charge of my contributions, all the holy gifts of the Sons of Israel. To you I have given them as a consecrated portion, and to your sons, as a perpetual allotment.

**22** This shall be yours from the most holy offerings, reserved from the fire: every offering of theirs -- every grain offering, every sin offering, and every guilt offering of theirs that they return to me -- shall be most holy, for you and for your sons.

**23** In the most holy place you shall eat it. Every male may eat it. It shall be holy to you.

**24** And this also is yours: the contribution of their gifts, along with all the wave offerings of the Sons of Israel. To you I have given them, and to your sons, and to your daughters with you, as a perpetual allotment. Everyone who is clean in your household may eat it.

**25** All the finest of the fresh oil, and all the finest of the new wine and grain -- their firstfruits, which they give to YHWH -- to you I have given them.

**26** The first ripe produce of everything in their land, which they bring to YHWH, shall be yours. Everyone who is clean in your household may eat it.

**27** Every devoted thing in Israel shall be yours.

**28** Every firstborn of every womb, of all flesh, which they bring to YHWH -- whether human or animal -- shall be yours. However, you must surely redeem the firstborn of a human being, and the firstborn of an unclean animal you shall redeem.

---

## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 2 -- "staff" (matteh):** The Hebrew *matteh* carries a deliberate double meaning: it is both a shepherd's/leader's staff (symbol of tribal authority) and the word for "tribe" itself. The test YHWH devises is elegant -- each tribe's *identity* is placed before him, and only one will show signs of life. The LXX (Vaticanus) uses *rhabdos* throughout, which captures the physical staff but loses the wordplay with tribal identity.

**Verse 5 -- "I will silence against myself" (vahashikoti me'alay):** The Hebrew is striking -- YHWH speaks of silencing the grumblings "from upon me." The complaints of Israel are experienced by YHWH as a weight pressing down on him. This is not divine indifference; it is a God burdened by his people's refusal to trust. I render "silence against myself" to capture the relational force.

**Verse 8 -- "sprouted...put forth buds...produced blossoms...borne ripe almonds":** The Hebrew piles four stages of growth into a single sentence: *parach* (sprouted), *wayyotse perach* (put out blossom), *wayyatsets tsits* (bloomed a bloom -- a cognate doubling), *wayyigmol sheqedim* (ripened almonds). This is an entire growing season compressed into one night -- an act of hyper-accelerated creation. The staff, dead wood cut from a living tree, has been resurrected into fruitfulness. The almond (*shaqed*) further echoes Jeremiah 1:11-12, where YHWH is the "watching one" (*shoqed*) -- the God who watches over his word to perform it. The Vaticanus text renders this sequence faithfully with *exeblastesen...exenegken blaston...exenthesen anthe...eblastesen karua*.

**Verse 10 -- "put an end, an utter end" (tekhalleh...telunnotam):** The verb *kalah* means to bring something to complete cessation. The doubled intensity in the construction conveys YHWH's exhaustion with the cycle of rebellion. Aaron's budded staff is not merely a trophy -- it is a permanent silencer, stored beside the Testimony as perpetual evidence.

**Verses 12-13 -- "We are perishing! We are lost!":** The Hebrew *gava'nu avadnu kullanu avadnu* is a cry of raw terror -- three rapid exclamations building in panic. The people have finally understood the deadly seriousness of approaching YHWH's presence without authorization. The question in verse 13 (*ha'im tamnu ligvo'a* -- "Are we finished off by dying?") is not rhetorical; it is genuine existential dread. This cry sets the stage for the priestly duties outlined in the following verses, which function as YHWH's compassionate answer: the priests and Levites will serve as a protective buffer between the holy and the common, so that Israel need not die.

**Verse 14 -- Chapter division note:** In the Leningrad Codex, what most English Bibles number as 17:14-28 corresponds to the beginning of chapter 18 in the Hebrew versification (18:1-15). The text flows without interruption in the manuscript. The priestly duties described here are YHWH's direct response to the people's terror in verses 12-13 -- the Aaronic priesthood exists not to elevate Aaron but to protect Israel from the lethal holiness of the sanctuary.

**Verse 19 -- "a gift given to you, dedicated to YHWH" (nettunim nettunim):** The doubled *nettunim* (given ones, given ones) is another cognate intensification. The Levites are emphatically, irrevocably given. They belong to Aaron's service not by their own choosing but by divine assignment. The LXX renders *doma dedomenous* -- "a gift that has been given" -- preserving the emphasis.

**Verse 20 -- "I give your priesthood as a gift of service":** The Hebrew *avodath mattanah* ("service of gift") reframes priesthood not as privilege but as gifted labor. The priesthood is simultaneously a burden (bearing Israel's guilt, v.14) and a grace (given by YHWH, not earned). This tension is central to the chapter's resolution of the Korah crisis.

**Verse 28 -- "you must surely redeem" (padoh tifdeh):** The infinitive absolute construction *padoh tifdeh* -- "redeeming you shall redeem" -- emphasizes the absolute obligation to redeem human firstborn. No human sacrifice is permitted in YHWH's system; the firstborn belongs to God but must be bought back. This is the economic and theological foundation of Israelite redemption theology.

-- PNEUMA
